stimulus: Twelve of Anton Chekhov's short stories are commonly classified as the major late tales. Although each one of these stories, which include The Lady with the Dog and Ward No. 6, ______ on a single protagonist (typically a middle-aged Russian struggling with disillusionment), a few, such as Peasants and In the Ravine, instead trace the entwined lives of several characters within one village.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-verb agreement. The singular verb "centers" agrees in number with the singular subject "each one of these stories," which refers to each story individually.

Choice A is incorrect because the plural verb "center" does not agree in number with the singular subject "each one of these stories." Choice C is incorrect because the plural verb "are centered" does not agree in number with the singular subject. Choice D is incorrect because the plural verb "were centered" does not agree in number with the singular subject.
